genre_refs: Howard Pyle 'The Nation Makers'; American Revolutionary War iconography
description: Slogan graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor that overlays text onto a painting. The artwork is Howard Pyle's 'The Nation Makers', showing a column of Revolutionary War Continental soldiers and militia moving across a field: men in white shirts and tricorn hats walk forward carrying muskets with fixed bayonets and shouldered rifles, one beating a red field drum, with a tattered American flag (early stars-and-stripes) and a uniformed officer at the left, painted in a warm painterly style under a smoke-streaked sky. Across the bottom of the image a small DOL eagle emblem sits above large white serif capital text reading 'PATRIOTISM, NOT GLOBALISM' set against a dark gradient bar. The tweet reads '2026 is the year of the Patriot. AMERICA FIRST. AMERICANS FIRST.'
